title: Implement azq agenda build as a thin command-layer writer
goal: Make azq/agenda/build.py coordinate exact parent-deliverable validation, deterministic stub task creation, and canonical Agenda writes without turning Agenda into an execution or orchestration engine.
context: Stage 3 defines Agenda as the stage that turns canonical Formam deliverables into executable task records and visible dependency structures.
Wave A already established canonical Agenda storage, normalization, parsing, exact deliverable validation, and write helpers under azq/agenda/.

The Stage 3 build document is partially stale relative to the live repo: azq/agenda already exists on disk, but Wave B should still follow its narrow command-layer intent rather than reopening storage work.
Agenda must remain subordinate to Formam rather than becoming a task runner, agent loop, or speculative planning layer.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/build.py.
Add a thin command-layer entrypoint such as build_agenda(deliverable_id).
Validate the parent deliverable by exact deliverable_id through the canonical Agenda or Formam validation helper.
Allocate the next stable TASK_### id through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Generate one deterministic stub task record from the validated deliverable data.
Ensure the generated canonical task record uses the Stage 3 task schema, especially description rather than an invented title field.
Write one canonical task file through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Keep the behavior minimal, inspectable, and free of execution-engine behavior, agent loops, or speculative orchestration logic.
acceptance_criteria: A valid canonical deliverable can produce one TASK_###.md file under data/agenda/tasks/.
The written task record conforms to the canonical Stage 3 task schema used by the Stage 3 build document.
build.py delegates persistence and file-format decisions to the existing Agenda storage helpers rather than writing files ad hoc.
The first build behavior remains intentionally small, deterministic, and human-readable.
suggested_commit_message: add agenda build command module for canonical tasks

title: Implement azq agenda list as a thin presentation layer
goal: Make azq/agenda/tasks.py provide a read-only listing view over canonical Agenda storage so built tasks become visible from the command layer.
context: The Stage 3 command loop should expose read commands only after the first canonical Agenda write path exists.
Wave A already made task files readable through the shared storage surface, so the list command should stay presentation-only.
The live repository has no Agenda command family yet, which makes a simple listing one of the first useful operator-visible behaviors.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/tasks.py.
Add a read-only listing function such as list_tasks().
Load canonical tasks through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Display at least task_id, deliverable_id, status, and a short readable description.
Use the canonical description field rather than assuming a title field exists on Agenda task records.
Keep the output simple, stable, and terminal-readable.
Preserve a clear no-tasks message when canonical Agenda storage is empty.
acceptance_criteria: tasks.py can present canonical Agenda tasks without mutating state.
The listing reads through shared storage helpers rather than duplicating repository logic.
The visible output stays focused on tasks, not execution, automation, or later-stage artifact behavior.

title: Implement azq agenda show with exact-id task inspection
goal: Extend azq/agenda/tasks.py so one canonical Agenda task can be inspected clearly by exact task_id.
context: The requested Wave B command set includes azq agenda show <task_id> as a first useful Agenda read command.
Exact-id lookup matters because later state changes will depend on stable TASK_### identifiers.
This command should inspect one existing task and stop there rather than broadening into task transitions, filtering, or log mutation.
requirements: Add a reader such as show_task(task_id).
Load one task by exact task_id through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Print the canonical task fields clearly, including deliverable_id, description, dependencies, status, execution_notes, and created.
Do not assume an Agenda task title field unless the live schema explicitly adds one.
Handle missing task ids with a readable exact-id failure message.
Keep output centered on canonical task inspection rather than execution behavior.
acceptance_criteria: One canonical task can be inspected by exact id.
Missing tasks fail clearly without fuzzy matching.
tasks.py remains a presentation module over shared storage helpers.
suggested_commit_message: add exact task inspection for agenda show

title: Implement azq agenda dag as a thin command-layer DAG refresher
goal: Make azq/agenda/dags.py generate or refresh a canonical Agenda DAG from the current deliverable-scoped task records for one exact deliverable while still writing the canonical goal-level DAG artifact for that deliverable's parent goal.
context: The requested Wave B command set includes azq agenda dag <deliverable_id> even though the Stage 3 build doc still describes goal-level dag commands; the live repo and operator request take precedence here.
Wave A already defined canonical DAG storage, normalization, parsing, and write helpers.
The DAG command should stay conservative and inspectable even if task dependency data is sparse.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/dags.py.
Add a command-layer entrypoint such as build_agenda_dag(deliverable_id) or agenda_dag(deliverable_id).
Validate the parent deliverable by exact deliverable_id through the canonical validation helper.
Load canonical tasks for that exact deliverable through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Resolve the parent goal from the validated deliverable and write or refresh one canonical GOAL_<goal_id>_DAG.json file through the existing Agenda DAG storage helpers.
If dependency edges are derived automatically, derive them conservatively from existing task dependency lists rather than inventing planning structure.
Do not introduce task execution, agent loops, log mutation, or smart orchestration behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A deliverable with canonical parent records can produce or refresh one visible DAG artifact.
The command is deliverable-scoped at the CLI layer but writes the canonical goal-level DAG artifact for the resolved parent goal.
DAG regeneration depends on canonical task records for the exact deliverable and the parent goal resolved from that deliverable.
dags.py remains a thin command-layer module over shared storage helpers.

title: Wire Agenda commands into the CLI
goal: Expose the first usable Agenda loop in the live router-based CLI without broadening beyond the Stage 3 Wave B command set.
context: The live CLI already routes Scintilla, Finis, and Formam through dedicated router modules, but Agenda has no router yet.
The requested Stage 3 Wave B command set is azq agenda build, azq agenda list, azq agenda show, and azq agenda dag.
The Stage 3 docs still describe azq task and azq dag command names in places, but the live repo and requested wave scope should follow the Agenda command family instead.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/cli.py and azq/agenda/router.py.
Add command help lines for azq agenda build <deliverable_id>, azq agenda list, azq agenda show <task_id>, and azq agenda dag <deliverable_id>.
Add an Agenda branch with clear usage messages for each supported subcommand.
Route build, list, show, and dag to the new azq/agenda modules.
Update azq/cli.py to dispatch through the new Agenda router alongside the existing engine routers.
Keep command handling narrow and stylistically consistent with the live router-based CLI.
Do not introduce task start, task complete, status, doctor, archive, or task-runner behavior in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes the first usable Agenda command loop.
azq agenda build, azq agenda list, azq agenda show, and azq agenda dag dispatch to dedicated Agenda modules.
CLI help and usage text stay aligned with the intentionally narrow Wave B scope.
